Name: Aerodactyl
Ability-Effect: No more Evolution cards can be played. This power stops working while Aerodactyl is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

Name: Articuno
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. If tails, this attack does 10 damage to each of your own Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Ditto
Ability-Effect: If Ditto is your Active Pokémon, treat it as if it were the same card as the Defending Pokémon, including type, Hit Points, Weakness, and so on, except Ditto can't evolve, always has this Pokémon Power, and you may treat any Energy attached to Ditto as Energy of any type. Ditto isn't a copy of any other Pokémon while Ditto is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

Name: Dragonite
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Dragonite is on your Bench, you may switch it with your Active Pokémon.
Effects: Flip 2 coins. This attack does 40 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Gengar
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your opponent's Pokémon to another (even if it would Knock Out the other Pokémon). This power can't be used if Gengar is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.
Effects: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 10 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Haunter
Ability-Effect: Whenever an attack does anything to Haunter, flip a coin. If heads, prevent all effects of that attack, including damage, done to Haunter. This power stops working while Haunter is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.
Effects: The Defending Pokémon is now Asleep.

Name: Hitmonlee
Effects: If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 20 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Hypno
Effects: Look at up to 3 cards from the top of either player's deck and rearrange them as you like.
If your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, choose 1 of them and this attack does 10 damage to it. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

Name: Kabutops
Effects: Remove a number of damage counters from Kabutops equal to half the damage done to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance) (rounded up to the nearest 10). If Kabutops has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

Name: Lapras
Effects: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to Lapras but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

Name: Magneton
Effects: Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for this attack. (Any other effects that would happen after applying Weakness and Resistance still happen.)
Does 20 damage to each Pokémon on each player's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Magneton does 100 damage to itself.

Name: Moltres
Effects: You may discard any number of [R] Energy cards attached to Moltres when you use this attack. If you do, discard that many cards from the top of your opponent's deck.
Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

Name: Muk
Ability-Effect: Ignore all Pokémon Powers other than Toxic Gases. This power stops working while Muk is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Raichu
Effects: Choose 3 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon and this attack does 10 damage to each of them. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) If your opponent has fewer than 3 Benched Pokémon, do the damage to each of them.

Name: Zapdos
Effects: For each of your opponent's Benched Pokémon, flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 20 damage to that Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Then, Zapdos does 10 damage times the number of tails to itself.

Name: Arbok
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads and if your opponent has any Benched Pokémon, he or she chooses 1 of them and switches it with the Defending Pokémon. (Do the damage before switching the Pokémon.)
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Cloyster
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed. If tails, this attack does nothing (not even damage).
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Gastly
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Put up to 2 Energy cards from your discard pile into your hand. Gastly does 10 damage to itself.

Name: Golbat
Effects: Remove a number of damage counters from Golbat equal to the damage done to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). If Golbat has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

Name: Golduck
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
If the Defending Pokémon has any Energy cards attached to it, choose 1 of them and discard it.

Name: Golem
Effects: Does 20 damage to each Pokémon on each player's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Golem does 100 damage to itself.

Name: Graveler
Effects: During your opponent's next turn, whenever 30 or less damage is done to Graveler (after applying Weakness and Resistance), prevent that damage. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

Name: Kingler
Effects: Does 10 damage times the number of damage counters on Kingler.

Name: Magmar
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Omastar
Effects: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to Omastar but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.
Flip 2 coins. This attack does 30 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Sandslash
Effects: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Seadra
Effects: Does 20 damage plus 10 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to Seadra but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.
Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Seadra.

Name: Slowbro
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move 1 damage counter from 1 of your Pokémon to Slowbro as long as you don't Knock Out Slowbro. This power can't be used if Slowbro is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

Name: Tentacruel
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Weezing
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Does 10 damage to each Pokémon on each player's Bench. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.) Weezing does 60 damage to itself.

Name: Ekans
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

Name: Geodude
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Grimer
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
All damage done by attacks to Grimer during your opponent's next turn is reduced by 20 (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

Name: Horsea
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon tries to attack during your opponent's next turn, your opponent flips a coin. If tails, that attack does nothing.

Name: Kabuto
Ability-Effect: Whenever an attack (even your own) does damage to Kabuto (after applying Weakness and Resistance), that attack does half the damage to Kabuto (rounded down to the nearest 10). (Any other effects of attacks still happen.) This power stops working while Kabuto is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

Name: Krabby
Effects: Search your deck for a Basic Pokémon named Krabby and put it onto your Bench. Shuffle your deck afterward. (You can't use this attack if your Bench if full.)

Name: Omanyte
Ability-Effect: Your opponent plays with his or her hand face up. This power stops working while Omanyte is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.
Effects: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each [W] Energy attached to Omanyte but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

Name: Psyduck
Effects: Your opponent can't play Trainer cards during his or her next turn.
Flip 3 coins. This attack does 10 damage times the number of heads.

Name: Shellder
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.
Flip a coin. If heads, prevent all damage done to Shellder during your opponent's next turn. (Any other effects of attacks still happen.)

Name: Slowpoke
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.
Discard a [P] Energy attached to this Pokémon. If you do, put an Item card from your discard pile into your hand.

Name: Tentacool
Ability-Effect: At any time during your turn (before your attack), you may return Tentacool to your hand. (Discard all cards attached to Tentacool.) This power can't be used the turn you put Tentacool into play or if Tentacool is Asleep, Confused, or Paralyzed.

Name: Zubat
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

Remove a number of damage counters from Zubat equal to the damage done to the Defending Pokémon (after applying Weakness and Resistance). If Zubat has fewer damage counters than that, remove all of them.

Name: Mr. Fuji
Description: Choose a Pokémon on your Bench. Shuffle it and any cards attached to it into your deck.

Name: Energy Search
Description: Search your deck for a Basic Energy card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

Name: Gambler
Description: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Flip a coin. If heads, draw 8 cards. If tails, draw 1 card.

Name: Recycle
Description: Flip a coin. If heads, put a card from your discard pile on top of your deck.

Name: Mysterious Fossil
Description: Play Mysterious Fossil as if it were a Basic Pokémon. While in play, Mysterious Fossil counts as a Pokémon (instead of a Trainer card). Mysterious Fossil has no attacks, can't retreat, and can't be Asleep, Confused, Paralyzed, or Poisoned. If Mysterious Fossil is Knocked Out, it doesn't count as a Knocked Out Pokémon. (Discard it anyway.) At any time during your turn before your attack, you may discard Mysterious Fossil from play.
